sources_audited.note: The cited Census interface is reachable and links to the official 2022 NAICS manual, whose 561492 entry defines verbatim reporting and stenotype recording of live legal proceedings, subsequent transcription, and real-time closed captioning.

O*NET lists the cited capture, proofreading, clarification, transcript production, storage, filing, exhibit, deposition, and oath tasks and reports exactness as extremely important for 96 percent of respondents.

The AAA page describes a U.S. deposition in which AI produced an immediate draft while a certified reporter administered the oath, monitored the record, reviewed it, and certified it; it also describes a virtual arbitration transcript proofed and edited by a human.

BLS reports 17,700 jobs in 2024, a 0 percent 2024-2034 projection, continued need for detailed legal records and human review of digital documentation, freelancer engagement by law firms and corporations, and hourly or per-page billing.

Veritext describes per-page transcript and exhibit charges, a flat litigation-package charge, expedited surcharges, rough-draft pricing, appearance fees, realtime charges, and reporter proofreading before finalization.
The dated U.S. Legal Support release announces its 2024 acquisition of Wendy Ward Roberts & Associates, a Dallas/Fort Worth court-reporting agency operating for more than 30 years.

NCRA's position statements characterize AI and ASR as supplemental, assert the need for trained certified human oversight in official-record creation and certification, and identify privacy, security, and confidentiality duties.

The Census page confirms an Age of Owner table for employer businesses and that the 2022 ABS covers reference year 2021, but the cited landing page supplies no narrowed-industry owner-age observation or transfer-rate denominator for s5.

caveats: All seven researched primitives are estimates. The reopened sources support the qualitative direction and boundary choices, but none directly measures the packet's numerical bases for automation exposure, realization, eligible-firm share, transfer probability, retained benefit, five-year service quantity, or operator persistence.
S3 is a provider-supported use case presented by AAA rather than representative adoption or quality evidence, so it establishes feasibility but not the prevalence assumed in rho.
S6 demonstrates that one longstanding agency transferred, while S8 only identifies an owner-age table; together they do not establish the s5 base of 0.18 or a sector-wide five-year transfer rate.

The BLS evidence is occupation-wide, includes government workers and captioners, and covers 2024-2034; translating it into the narrowed private-agency five-year d5 range remains a judgment, and the 0.92 downside may underrepresent the packet's own stated digital-recording substitution risk.
The q base of 0.45 is inferred from fee architecture rather than observed contracts, discounts, margins, or customer response to AI-assisted delivery.

The a and rho rationales both invoke certification, difficult audio, and retained human review. Stage 2 should separate technical task exposure from implementation constraints to avoid discounting the same limitation twice; the record does not establish that resolving this boundary would change the base tier or action, so this is not material under the frozen test.
The legal-reporting-only lens is a disclosed and coherent narrowing of a heterogeneous NAICS code, but the resulting estimates should not be generalized to standalone captioning firms.
summary: Mechanics pass and every cited source was reopened. The disclosed legal-reporting lens is coherent, evidence states are honest, factor semantics and narrative conclusions are directionally consistent, and no identified defect is shown to change the LOW_PRIORITY base tier or VALIDATE_ASSUMPTIONS action. Publication is appropriate with caveats because the record is useful but extensively model-conditioned, especially for transferability, value capture, demand translation, and the separation of automation exposure from implementation constraints.
